Mission: Obtain affordable, nutritious, convenient, and timely meals that support learning and well-being
Resources: Money, time, appetite, dietary preferences, mobility, information access
Expected Outputs: Meal demand, payment, feedback, preference signals, consumption behavior
Value Generated: Health, satisfaction, learning readiness, campus participation, demand information for vendors
Objectives: Maximize satisfaction, nutrition, affordability, convenience, and reduced waiting time
Constraints: Budget, class schedule, queue time, dietary restrictions, menu availability
Decision Variables: Meal choice, vendor choice, order time, budget allocation, feedback submission
Relevant RMOV Accounts: R: budget/time; M: meal acquisition; O: demand/feedback; V: satisfaction/health

Mission: Prepare and sell meals while maintaining financial viability, service quality, and campus mission alignment
Resources: Ingredients, labor, kitchen equipment, working capital, menu knowledge, vendor space
Expected Outputs: Prepared meals, service capacity, menu information, sales data, waste data
Value Generated: Profit, employment, food availability, service quality, contribution to campus welfare
Objectives: Maximize profit, demand fulfillment, utilization, food quality, and customer satisfaction while minimizing waste
Constraints: Capacity, ingredient availability, labor availability, food safety rules, cost inflation, demand uncertainty
Decision Variables: Production quantity, menu mix, price, opening hours, staffing, procurement quantity
Relevant RMOV Accounts: R: ingredients/labor/capital; M: food production; O: meals; V: profit/reputation
Relevant PSKVE Exchanges: Receives Product/Knowledge; provides Product/Service; generates Environmental impact

Mission: Deliver reliable, affordable, and quality inputs to vendors according to ecosystem demand
Resources: Raw materials, transport capacity, inventory, supplier network, market price information
Expected Outputs: Ingredient deliveries, availability information, price signals, supply reliability
Value Generated: Supply continuity, vendor readiness, reduced shortage risk, supplier revenue
Objectives: Maximize delivery reliability, sales volume, inventory turnover, and supplier margin
Constraints: Stock availability, logistics capacity, lead time, price volatility, perishability
Decision Variables: Delivery quantity, delivery timing, price, inventory allocation, sourcing decision
Relevant RMOV Accounts: R: stock/logistics; M: supply delivery; O: delivered ingredients; V: revenue/reliability

Mission: Move meals or supplies efficiently between stakeholders when required by the operating model
Resources: Vehicles, riders, routing information, time, fuel or energy, delivery platform access
Expected Outputs: Delivered meals, delivered ingredients, delivery time data, service availability
Value Generated: Convenience, reduced travel time, logistics flexibility, improved access to food
Objectives: Maximize delivery reliability, route efficiency, service coverage, and income while minimizing delay
Constraints: Route congestion, delivery capacity, weather, fuel cost, service radius, time windows
Decision Variables: Route selection, delivery assignment, delivery timing, fleet allocation
Relevant RMOV Accounts: R: vehicles/time/energy; M: delivery service; O: completed deliveries; V: fee/reliability
Relevant PSKVE Exchanges: Provides Service; receives Value; affects Environment

Expected Outputs: Policies, subsidies, facility allocation, mission targets, evaluation criteria

Constraints: Budget, regulations, fairness, institutional priorities, infrastructure limits

Relevant RMOV Accounts: R: budget/facilities/authority; M: campus welfare governance; O: policies/subsidies; V: mission achievement
Relevant PSKVE Exchanges: Provides Value/Knowledge/Environment governance; receives Knowledge/mission outcomes
